content:
## MARKET OVERVIEW

1. What is the Current Market Regime?
Neutral regime — normal volatility (Vol: 26.6%) with very narrow participation: breadth 0/8 sectors positive, so downside is broad despite stable vol.

2. Which sectors are leading?
Energy & Chemicals is the relative leader but still BELOW TREND: TUPRS (1W: +18.08% | 1M: +13.91%) ABOVE TREND; Heavy Industry is second-best but BELOW TREND: ASELS (1W: +3.57% | 1M: +17.02%) ABOVE TREND at stock level.

3. Which sectors are lagging?
Banking is the weakest and BELOW TREND with steep losses across names; Technology & Telecom and Transportation also lag and are BELOW TREND.

## STOCK PERFORMANCE

4. Top Performing Stocks?
TUPRS (1W: +18.08% | 1M: +13.91%) ABOVE TREND; PETKM (1W: +6.42% | 1M: +3.08%) ABOVE TREND; ASELS (1W: +3.57% | 1M: +17.02%) ABOVE TREND; TKFEN (1W: +2.57% | 1M: -9.79%) RECOVERY; CCOLA (1W: -1.12% | 1M: -4.28%) BELOW TREND.

5. Worst Performing Stocks?
GARAN (1W: -15.37% | 1M: -12.18%) BELOW TREND; ISCTR (1W: -15.91% | 1M: -14.23%) BELOW TREND; VAKBN (1W: -16.36% | 1M: -6.98%) BELOW TREND; AKBNK (1W: -17.86% | 1M: -12.26%) BELOW TREND; YKBNK (1W: -18.34% | 1M: -7.58%) BELOW TREND.

6. Momentum Stocks?
Strongest 1W movers include YKBNK (1W: -18.34% | 1M: -7.58%) BELOW TREND, AKBNK (1W: -17.86% | 1M: -12.26%) BELOW TREND, TUPRS (1W: +18.08% | 1M: +13.91%) ABOVE TREND, ISCTR (1W: -15.91% | 1M: -14.23%) BELOW TREND and VAKBN (1W: -16.36% | 1M: -6.98%) BELOW TREND.

## SECTOR OUTLOOK

7. Banking Sector Outlook?
Banking is under pressure and clearly BELOW TREND; focus on AKBNK (1W: -17.86% | 1M: -12.26%) BELOW TREND, GARAN (1W: -15.37% | 1M: -12.18%) BELOW TREND and ISCTR (1W: -15.91% | 1M: -14.23%) BELOW TREND for downside risk and liquidity stress signals.

8. Industrial Sector Outlook?
Heavy Industry is weakening and overall BELOW TREND despite pockets of strength: ASELS (1W: +3.57% | 1M: +17.02%) ABOVE TREND vs EREGL (1W: -13.66% | 1M: -1.74%) BELOW TREND and ARCLK (1W: -5.38% | 1M: -9.92%) BELOW TREND.

9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?
Energy & Chemicals is the best relative group but still BELOW TREND as a sector; watch TUPRS (1W: +18.08% | 1M: +13.91%) ABOVE TREND, PETKM (1W: +6.42% | 1M: +3.08%) ABOVE TREND and TRMET (1W: -13.21% | 1M: +12.87%) CORRECTION for follow-through and oil/FX sensitivity.

## PORTFOLIO STRATEGY

10. Portfolio Recommendation?
Neutral tactical stance: reduce exposure to banks/levered cyclicals, trim market-weight names failing to hold 1M, selectively upweight momentum pockets (TUPRS, ASELS) while keeping cash/hedges ready given breadth at 0/8.

## NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK

11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?
TUPRS (1W: +18.08% | 1M: +13.91%) ABOVE TREND for continuation or fade; ASELS (1W: +3.57% | 1M: +17.02%) ABOVE TREND for momentum; AKBNK (1W: -17.86% | 1M: -12.26%) BELOW TREND and ISCTR (1W: -15.91% | 1M: -14.23%) BELOW TREND for potential short-cover or further downside; TTKOM (1W: -13.02% | 1M: -12.64%) BELOW TREND as a risk-on/test name.

12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?
Banking for any capitulation/re-pricing, Energy & Chemicals for momentum and oil/FX sensitivity, and Technology for downside continuation.

13. Key Levels & Triggers?
Watch macro and market triggers: TRYFX moves, CBRT guidance, oil price swings and Q4/early 2026 earnings; on index action, monitor breach of recent short-term support which would amplify selling, while sustained recovery in oil/FX/earnings could validate TUPRS and PETKM strength.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Neutral regime with normal vol but zero sector breadth — broad selling dominated by banks and tech while energy names (notably TUPRS) and ASELS show concentrated strength. Tactical posture: neutral overall, rotate into confirmed momentum winners but keep banking exposure limited and watch macro/currency catalysts.
